seoTitle: How to Make Two Images the Same Size in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to resize two images to matching dimensions in Canva for consistent, professional-looking designs.
intro: Mismatched image sizes can make a design look unpolished, especially for branded content. This tutorial shows how to resize two images to the same dimensions in Canva, keeping your layouts consistent and professional.

steps.heading: Select the images to resize
steps.body: Bring both images into your Canva project so you can compare and adjust their dimensions.

steps.heading: Resize to match dimensions
steps.body: Adjust the width and height of each image so they align to the same size.

steps.heading: Maintain quality during resizing
steps.body: Check that resizing doesn't distort or blur the images, especially if you're scaling up.

steps.heading: Apply consistent sizing for branding
steps.body: Use matched image sizes across marketing materials or social posts for a more polished, professional look.

faq.question: Will resizing images in Canva reduce their quality?
faq.answer: Resizing down generally preserves quality well, but scaling a smaller image up can cause some blurriness.

faq.question: Is there a quick way to match sizes without manual dragging?
faq.answer: Yes, entering exact width and height values for both images is more precise than dragging by hand.

faq.question: Can I lock the aspect ratio while resizing?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva typically allows you to lock proportions so resizing doesn't distort the image shape.

faq.question: Why does consistent image sizing matter for branding?
faq.answer: Uniform sizing creates a cleaner, more professional appearance across your marketing materials and social content.
